description_long: This sculpture depicts a seated figure with the head of a lioness, reminiscent of the ancient Egyptian goddess Sekhmet. The figure is rendered in a blue-green patina, suggesting it is made of faience or glazed ceramic, and features detailed facial features, a mane, and stylized body proportions. The figure sits upright on a rectangular base, with its hands resting on its knees and its legs drawn up. The back of the sculpture is rounded and smooth, with a visible number inscribed, likely for cataloging. The surface shows signs of age, with areas of discoloration and wear, adding to its historical character. The overall form is compact and solid, with no visible openings or moving parts.
description: A blue-green seated lioness-headed figure, likely inspired by ancient Egyptian art, with detailed facial features and a compact, solid form.
description_view_0: Front view showing the lioness head, detailed facial features, and both hands resting on the knees.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the lioness head, mane, and the seated posture with bent knees.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth back, mane, and an inscribed number on the lower back.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the profile of the head, mane, and the arm and leg positioning similar to the left side.
attribute_subject_matter: seated lioness-headed figure, likely inspired by ancient Egyptian iconography
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
